<!-- AUTO-GENERATED BY CONCERT v0.1.0 — DO NOT EDIT. This file is managed by Concert and will be overwritten on `concert update`. -->

# Concert Workflow: Execution

This workflow governs the entire execution phase — from starting phase 1 through completing all phases.

## Phase Execution Order

1. Phases execute in numerical order (01, 02, 03, ...)
2. Within each phase, tasks execute in wave order (wave 1 before wave 2)
3. Within a wave, tasks with no mutual dependencies can run in parallel (GitHub Actions) or sequentially (Claude Code)
4. Dependencies declared in task frontmatter (`depends_on`) must be complete before starting the task

## Task Execution Loop (per task)

1. Read task file frontmatter: model tier, depends_on, wave
2. Verify all `depends_on` tasks are complete
3. Execute through the code quality loop (CONCERT-WORKFLOW-CODE-QUALITY.md)
4. On success: update state.json (tasks_completed++, telemetry record, history entry)
5. On failure: write failure block to state.json, stop immediately
6. Update PHASE-SUMMARY after each task file completes

## State Management Rules

- `state.json` is updated and committed after every task completion
- `current_phase`, `current_task_file`, `current_task_index` track position
- `quality_loop_state` tracks mid-loop position for crash recovery
- `quality_loop_state` is cleared after task completion

## Phase Completion

1. After all tasks in a phase complete: spawn concert-documenter
2. Documentation agent updates higher-level docs (README, API docs, etc.)
3. Mark phase complete in state.json (phases_completed++)
4. Update WIP PR body

## Failure Handling

1. On failure: write failure block with `error_type`, `error_summary`, `files_touched`, `last_successful_commit`
2. Append to `failure_log`
3. Stop execution immediately — no skipping ahead
4. Recovery path:
   - Simple failures: `concert-debug` to diagnose and fix
   - `concert-continue` to retry after fix

## Context Compaction Rules

- Current phase: read TASK files + PHASE-SUMMARY
- Previous phase: read PHASE-SUMMARY only
- Older phases: skip unless specifically relevant
- Orchestrator keeps context to ~15% of window

## Commit Format

- Each task produces exactly one conventional commit: `type(scope): description`
- State.json update is included in the task commit

## Cross-Environment Handoff

1. If session stops: state.json reflects last committed task
2. User runs `npx @he3-org/concert push` to push to origin
3. `concert-continue` in the other environment resumes from state.json
4. See concert-push agent for handoff instructions
